candidate.name: Taylor Westfall
candidate.location_preference: Flexible. Remote preferred; on-site/hybrid OK for the right role. Open to relocation.
target_titles: Director, AI Quality / QA for AI
QA Lead (only if 'Senior'/'Principal' and people-management scope)
candidate.scope: QA/Quality leadership. Primary: video game studios. Secondary: QA/QE leadership at any tech company (SaaS, fintech, etc.).

resume.profile_for_fit: Director-level QA & Localization leader, 20+ yrs. Current: Director, QA & Localization @ Fortis Games. Prior: Head of Publishing QA @ Netflix; Head of Publishing QA + Program Manager QA Automation/Tools + QA Manager @ Amazon Games (New World, Crucible, Lost Ark, Lumberyard); Test Manager II @ Blizzard (Overwatch, Battle.net, Diablo 3). Strengths: building QA orgs from scratch, CI/CD & test automation strategy, live-service/LiveOps & incident management, multi-platform (Mobile, PC, Xbox, PlayStation, Mac), vendor management, staffing & budget ownership, localization, ISTQB Advanced Test Manager (CTAL-TM). BS Media Arts & Animation. Based Orange County, CA. KEY DIFFERENTIATOR (2026): leading Fortis' AI transformation of QA — using LLMs and agentic AI tools to automate test workflows and enable QA staff to build their own automation/optimization tech, targeting an 80% reduction in manual release-test time. Strong on agentic AI & LLM-driven test automation and AI workflow/tooling design.
resume.fit_rubric: Score 0-100. Excellent (90-100): games + Director/Head, live-service or automation focus. Strong (80-89): games leadership slightly above/below his level (e.g. Senior QA Manager), OR any role centered on AI/LLM-driven test-automation transformation. Good (65-79): plain QA Manager (under-leveled) OR strong non-games QA-leadership at SaaS/games-adjacent. Stretch (50-64): non-games domain shift (fintech) or VP-level reach. Low (<50): likely non-software quality (e.g. pharma/manufacturing QA) or scope mismatch. AI BONUS: add +5 to +10 when a role explicitly wants AI/LLM/agentic-driven testing, test-automation modernization, or QE-tooling transformation — Taylor now leads exactly this. When AI/automation is central, weave the '80% manual-test reduction via agentic AI' story into the blurb. Generate a 1-line 'why' and a 2-sentence first-person cover-letter blurb tailored to the role.

job_schema.notes_on_fields: salary = comp range if posted (e.g. '$150k-$190k'). date_posted = when the role went live, if shown. closed = true if the posting now 404s/closed (dashboard dims it). Auto-archive: dashboard flags a role 'stale' if still un-actioned 30+ days after date_found.
